mindxlib.pre_mining package¶

Submodules¶

mindxlib.pre_mining.extract_rule module¶

mindxlib.pre_mining.extract_rule.extract_fpgrowth(X, minsupport=0.1, maxcardinality=2, verbose=False) → List[Tuple]¶

extract frequent itemsets as rules from a one-hot dataframe

:param

X: pandas DataFrame, shape (n_samples, n_features), with onehot encoded format 0/1.

:param
minsupport: float, between 0 and 1 for minimum support of the itemsets returned.

The support is computed as the fraction transactions_where_item(s)_occur / total_transactions.

:param
maxcardinality: Maximum length of the itemsets generated. If None (default) all

possible itemsets lengths are evaluated.

Parameters

verbose –

Returns

list of itemsets

mindxlib.pre_mining.extract_rule.extract_rf(X, y, n_trees=100)¶

extract rules from random forest :param X: pandas DataFrame, shape (n_samples, n_features) :param y: shape (n_samples,)

The target values (class labels in classification, real numbers in regression).

Parameters

n_trees – int, default=100 The number of trees in the forest.

Returns

mindxlib.pre_mining.extract_rule.extract_rules_from_tree(clf, X)¶

extract rule from a single tree, which is already trained :param clf: tree estimator :param X: pandas DataFrame, shape (n_samples, n_features) :return: list of rules

mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule module¶

mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.InitCentroids(X, K)¶
class mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.WKModes(n_clusters=3, max_iter=100, lambda_=7.0, gamma=1)¶

Bases: object

fit(X)¶
fit_predict(X, y=None)¶
get_centers()¶
get_cost()¶
get_params()¶
get_weight()¶
mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.computeCentroidsAndWeights(X, centroids, labels, K, lambda_)¶
mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.extract_wkmodes(data_df, label_col, label_val, pos_k=None, neg_k=None, lambda_=0.1)¶

extract rules based on weighted kmodes, which is a clustering method

Parameters

  • data_df – pandas DataFrame, including features and label as columns

  • label_col – column name of label

  • label_val – value of positive class in label_col

  • pos_k – user-defined num of clusters in positive samples, default None

  • neg_k – user-defined num of clusters in negative samples, default None

  • lambda – default 0.1, control the entropy of feature weights bigger value results in more uniform distribution of weights

Returns

mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.findClosestCentroids(X, w, centroids, gamma)¶
mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.gen_batches(n, batch_size, *, min_batch_size=0)¶
mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.get_chunk_n_rows(row_bytes, *, max_n_rows=None, working_memory=None)¶
mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.get_unique(input_list)¶
mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.init_cao(X, n_clusters, dissim)¶

Initialize centroids according to method by Cao et al. [2009]. Note: O(N * attr * n_clusters**2), so watch out with large n_clusters

mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.init_huang(X, n_clusters, dissim, random_state)¶

Initialize centroids according to method by Huang [1997].

mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.init_random(X, n_clusters)¶

mindxlib.pre_mining.wkmodes_rule.matching_dissim(a, b)¶
